Objective: Provide financial income support to eligible farmer families.
Description: PM-KISAN provides eligible farmer families with direct financial assistance through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). The amount is credited in installments to help farmers manage agricultural expenses and improve their livelihood.
Eligibility: Indian landholding farmer families with cultivable land as per state records. Certain categories such as institutional landholders and some income tax payers are excluded.
Benefits: ₹6,000 per year in three equal DBT installments to eligible farmer families for agricultural expenses.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Land Ownership Records, Bank Account Details, Mobile Number
Income Limit: No fixed income limit (eligible landholding farmers; exclusions apply)
Keywords: farmer, agriculture, income support, DBT, farming, cultivator, financial assistance, crops

Objective: Provide affordable credit to farmers for agricultural activities.
Description: Kisan Credit Card offers farmers easy access to short-term loans for farming, livestock, and fisheries. It provides credit at affordable interest rates to support agricultural activities and reduce dependence on informal lenders.
Eligibility: Farmers engaged in agriculture, animal husbandry, dairy, or fisheries with eligible landholding or farming activities. Applicants must meet the lending institution's requirements.
Benefits: Low-interest crop loans, flexible credit, and access to working capital for farming, dairy, fisheries, and allied activities.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card (if required), Land Records/Lease Documents, Passport-size Photo, Bank Account Details
Keywords: farmer loan, agriculture loan, crop loan, dairy, fisheries, credit, farming, bank loan

Objective: Protect farmers against crop losses due to natural calamities.
Description: PMFBY provides insurance coverage against crop losses caused by natural disasters, pests, and diseases. The scheme helps farmers recover financially and continue agricultural production after unforeseen events.
Eligibility: Farmers growing notified crops in notified areas who have an insurable interest in the crop. Both loanee and eligible non-loanee farmers can enroll, subject to current scheme rules.
Benefits: Financial compensation for crop losses due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Land Records, Bank Passbook, Sowing Certificate (if applicable), Passport-size Photo
Keywords: crop insurance, crop loss, natural disaster, farmer insurance, compensation, agriculture, farming

Objective: Promote balanced fertilizer use by providing soil health information.
Description: This scheme provides farmers with information about the nutrient status of their soil and recommendations for balanced fertilizer use. It encourages sustainable farming practices and improves crop productivity.
Eligibility: All farmers are eligible to receive soil testing and a Soil Health Card through the designated government programme.
Benefits: Free soil testing, personalized nutrient recommendations, and improved crop productivity through balanced fertilizer use.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Land Records, Farmer ID (if applicable)
Keywords: soil testing, soil health, fertilizer, nutrients, agriculture, farming, crop productivity

Objective: Improve irrigation facilities and ensure efficient water use in agriculture.
Description: PMKSY aims to improve irrigation coverage and promote efficient water management in agriculture. It encourages water conservation and the adoption of modern irrigation techniques such as drip and sprinkler systems.
Eligibility: Farmers, farmer groups, cooperatives, and eligible organizations involved in agriculture or irrigation projects, subject to component-specific guidelines.
Benefits: Improved irrigation facilities, efficient water use, and financial support for modern irrigation systems.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Land Records, Bank Account Details, Identity Proof, Project Documents (if applicable)
Keywords: irrigation, water conservation, drip irrigation, sprinkler, farming, agriculture, water management

Objective: Provide cashless health insurance to economically vulnerable families.
Description: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY offers eligible families cashless health insurance coverage for hospitalization at empanelled hospitals. It reduces the financial burden of medical treatment and improves access to quality healthcare.
Eligibility: Families identified as eligible under the scheme's beneficiary database (based on government criteria). Beneficiaries must verify their eligibility before availing benefits.
Benefits: Cashless hospitalization coverage of up to ₹5 lakh per eligible family per year at empanelled hospitals.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card (or other valid ID), Ration Card/Family ID (if applicable), Mobile Number
Income Limit: As per government beneficiary list (SECC/eligible database)
Keywords: health insurance, hospital, cashless treatment, healthcare, medical, PMJAY, hospitalization

Objective: Deliver comprehensive primary healthcare services to all citizens.
Description: Health & Wellness Centres provide comprehensive primary healthcare services, including preventive, promotive, and basic curative care. They improve access to essential healthcare services, especially in rural areas.
Eligibility: Available to all citizens seeking primary healthcare services at Health & Wellness Centres. No specific income or age restriction for basic services.
Benefits: Free primary healthcare services, essential medicines, diagnostic tests, maternal and child healthcare, and preventive care.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card or any Government-issued ID (if required)
Keywords: primary healthcare, health centre, free treatment, medicines, diagnostics, wellness, healthcare

Objective: Improve access to quality healthcare through financial protection
Description: PM-JAY is a flagship health insurance scheme that provides financial protection for secondary and tertiary healthcare services. It enables eligible beneficiaries to receive cashless treatment at participating hospitals across India.
Eligibility: Eligible families identified under the scheme's approved beneficiary list. Eligibility is determined by government-defined socio-economic criteria.
Benefits: Cashless treatment for secondary and tertiary healthcare services at empanelled hospitals with financial protection against major medical expenses.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Family ID/Ration Card (if applicable), Mobile Number
Keywords: health insurance, cashless treatment, hospitalization, medical expenses, healthcare, PMJAY

Objective: Reduce maternal and infant mortality by promoting institutional deliveries.
Description: Janani Suraksha Yojana encourages pregnant women to opt for institutional deliveries by providing financial assistance. The scheme aims to reduce maternal and infant mortality and improve maternal healthcare services.
Eligibility: Pregnant women meeting the scheme guidelines, particularly those from eligible low-income households and delivering in government or accredited health facilities.
Benefits: Cash assistance to eligible pregnant women for institutional delivery and improved maternal healthcare.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Pregnancy Registration Card, Bank Account Details, Mother & Child Protection Card
Income Limit: Varies by state/category (priority to eligible low-income women)
Keywords: pregnancy, maternity, institutional delivery, pregnant women, childbirth, maternal health

Objective: Ensure quality antenatal care for pregnant women.
Description: This scheme provides free and quality antenatal healthcare services to pregnant women on designated days each month. It promotes early detection and management of high-risk pregnancies to ensure safer motherhood.
Eligibility: All pregnant women, especially those in the second and third trimesters, are eligible to receive free antenatal check-ups at participating government health facilities.
Benefits: Free antenatal check-ups, essential diagnostic tests, specialist consultation, and identification of high-risk pregnancies.
Required Documents: Pregnancy Registration Card/Mother & Child Protection Card, Aadhaar Card (if required), Medical Records
Keywords: pregnancy, antenatal care, maternal health, prenatal checkup, pregnant women, doctor

Objective: Provide affordable housing for eligible urban families
Description: PMAY-U helps eligible urban families own affordable and permanent houses through financial assistance and interest subsidies on home loans. It focuses on providing housing for economically weaker sections, low-income groups, and middle-income groups.
Eligibility: Economically Weaker Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG), Middle Income Group (MIG), or other eligible urban households that do not own a pucca house, subject to scheme guidelines.
Benefits: Financial assistance and interest subsidy for purchasing, constructing, or improving a house under eligible components.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Address Proof, Bank Account Details, Property Documents (if applicable)
Keywords: housing, home loan, affordable housing, urban housing, subsidy, house

Objective: Provide permanent housing to eligible rural households.
Description: PMAY-G provides financial assistance to eligible rural households for constructing durable houses with basic amenities. It aims to improve living conditions and ensure "Housing for All" in rural areas.
Eligibility: Rural households identified through the government's approved beneficiary list (SECC data and updated criteria) who do not own a permanent house.
Benefits: Financial assistance for construction of a pucca house with basic amenities in rural areas.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, Job Card (if applicable), Residence Proof
Income Limit: Identified eligible rural households (not a fixed income limit)
Keywords: rural housing, house construction, pucca house, village, housing assistance

Objective: Ensure financial security after retirement for workers in the unorganized sector.
Description: APY is a voluntary pension scheme for workers in the unorganized sector. Subscribers contribute regularly during their working years and receive a guaranteed monthly pension after reaching the age of 60.
Eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18–40 years with a savings bank account who contribute regularly until the age of 60.
Benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension (₹1,000–₹5,000) after the age of 60, based on contributions made during the subscription period.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Savings Bank Account, Mobile Number
Keywords: pension, retirement, old age, savings, monthly pension, APY

Scheme Name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
Objective: Provide affordable life insurance coverage to citizens.
Description: PMJJBY is an affordable life insurance scheme that provides financial support to the nominee in case of the insured person's death. It offers annual renewable coverage at a low premium.
Eligibility: Indian residents aged 18–50 years with a savings bank account and consent for annual premium auto-debit.
Benefits: ₹2 lakh life insurance cover to the nominee in case of the insured person's death.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Savings Bank Account, Auto-debit Consent Form
Keywords: life insurance, insurance, death benefit, nominee, PMJJBY

Objective: Provide affordable accident insurance coverage.
Description: PMSBY is an accident insurance scheme that provides financial assistance in case of accidental death or disability. It offers annual coverage at a highly affordable premium.
Eligibility: Indian residents aged 18–70 years with a savings bank account and consent for annual premium auto-debit.
Benefits: ₹2 lakh on accidental death or total permanent disability; ₹1 lakh for partial permanent disability.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Savings Bank Account, Auto-debit Consent Form
Keywords: accident insurance, disability, accidental death, PMSBY, insurance

Objective: Provide financial assistance to elderly, widows, and persons with disabilities.
Description: NSAP provides financial assistance to elderly persons, widows, and persons with disabilities belonging to economically weaker families. It aims to strengthen social security and improve their quality of life.
Eligibility: Individuals from Below Poverty Line (BPL) households who meet the age, disability, or widowhood criteria under the specific NSAP component.
Benefits: Monthly financial assistance to eligible elderly persons, widows, and persons with disabilities.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Income/BPL Certificate, Bank Account Details, Age Proof (or Disability/Widow Certificate, if applicable)
Income Limit: BPL/EWS households as per government criteria
Age Criteria: Varies (60+, widows, or persons with disabilities depending on component)
Keywords: pension, widow pension, disability pension, senior citizen, financial assistance

Objective: Support traditional artisans and craftspeople through financial and skill assistance.
Description: PM Vishwakarma supports traditional artisans and craftspeople by providing skill training, financial assistance, modern tools, and market access. It aims to improve productivity and preserve traditional occupations.
Eligibility: Traditional artisans and craftspeople engaged in one of the notified trades and meeting the scheme's age and occupational criteria.
Benefits: Skill training, toolkit incentive, concessional loans, digital incentives, and marketing support for traditional artisans.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Mobile Number, Bank Account Details, Occupation Proof (if applicable)
Keywords: artisan, craftsman, toolkit, traditional worker, skill training, loan

Objective: Promote entrepreneurship by providing collateral-free business loans.
Description: PMMY provides collateral-free loans to small businesses and entrepreneurs through banks and financial institutions. The scheme supports business expansion, self-employment, and income generation.
Eligibility: Individuals, startups, and micro or small business owners seeking loans for non-farm income-generating activities, subject to bank approval.
Benefits: Collateral-free business loans up to ₹20 lakh (under current scheme categories) to support micro and small enterprises
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Bank Account Details, Business Proof/Plan, Address Proof
Keywords: business loan, MSME, startup loan, entrepreneur, Mudra, collateral-free loan

Objective: Encourage entrepreneurship among women and SC/ST entrepreneurs.
Description: Stand-Up India promotes entrepreneurship among women and SC/ST entrepreneurs by providing bank loans for setting up new enterprises in manufacturing, services, and trading sectors.
Eligibility: SC/ST entrepreneurs or women entrepreneurs aged 18 years or above starting a new (greenfield) enterprise in manufacturing, services, trading, or agriculture-related activities.
Benefits: Bank loans ranging from ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore for setting up new enterprises, along with handholding and support services.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Business Plan, Bank Account Details, Caste Certificate (if applicable)
Keywords: SC entrepreneur, ST entrepreneur, women entrepreneur, startup, business loan

Objective: Promote innovation and support startup businesses in India.
Description: Startup India supports entrepreneurs by promoting innovation, simplifying regulations, and providing access to funding, mentorship, and incubation. It aims to build a strong startup ecosystem and encourage job creation.
Eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startups incorporated as a private limited company, partnership firm, or LLP, meeting the government's startup eligibility criteria.
Benefits: Tax benefits, easier compliance, funding support, incubation, mentorship, and access to government initiatives for eligible startups.
Required Documents: PAN Card, Certificate of Incorporation, DPIIT Recognition, Business Documents
Keywords: startup, innovation, entrepreneur, funding, business, incubation

Scheme Name: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
Objective: Improve credit availability to micro and small enterprises without collateral.
Description: CGTMSE helps micro and small enterprises obtain collateral-free loans by providing credit guarantees to lending institutions. It improves access to finance for businesses that lack sufficient assets for loan security.
Eligibility: New or existing Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) obtaining eligible collateral-free loans through member lending institutions.
Benefits: Credit guarantee for collateral-free loans, improving access to finance for Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs).
Required Documents: Business Registration Documents, Loan Application, PAN Card, Aadhaar Card
Keywords: collateral-free loan, MSME, credit guarantee, business finance, enterprise

Objective: Generate self-employment opportunities through financial assistance.
Description: PMEGP promotes self-employment by providing financial assistance to individuals for establishing micro-enterprises in manufacturing and service sectors. It supports entrepreneurship and employment generation, especially in rural and semi-urban areas.
Eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18 years or above seeking financial assistance to establish a new micro-enterprise. Educational qualification may apply for projects above the prescribed cost.
Benefits: Financial assistance in the form of subsidy for setting up new micro-enterprises and generating self-employment opportunities.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Project Report, Educational Certificate (if applicable), Bank Account Details, Caste Certificate (if applicable)
Keywords: self-employment, business subsidy, entrepreneur, micro enterprise, startup

Objective: Enable nationwide portability of ration benefits for eligible beneficiaries.
Description: ONORC allows eligible ration card holders to access subsidized food grains from any Fair Price Shop across India. The scheme ensures food security for migrant workers and their families by making ration benefits portable.
Eligibility: Beneficiaries holding a valid ration card under the National Food Security Act (NFSA).
Benefits: Access to subsidized food grains from any Fair Price Shop across India using the same ration card.
Required Documents: NFSA Ration Card, Aadhaar Card
Keywords: ration card, portability, food grains, PDS, migrant workers

Objective: Provide highly subsidized food grains to the poorest households.
Description: AAY provides highly subsidized food grains to the poorest households in the country. It aims to reduce hunger and ensure food security for the most vulnerable sections of society.
Eligibility: Poorest and most vulnerable households identified by the respective State/UT Governments under the AAY guidelines.
Benefits: Highly subsidized food grains for the poorest households to improve food security.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Income/BPL Certificate
Income Limit: Poorest households identified by State Government
Keywords: ration, food security, poor families, subsidized food, AAY

Objective: Ensure food and nutritional security for eligible households.
Description: Under the NFSA, eligible households receive subsidized food grains through the Public Distribution System (PDS). The scheme aims to improve food and nutritional security across the country.
Eligibility: Households identified by State/UT Governments as eligible under the National Food Security Act.
Benefits: Subsidized food grains through the Public Distribution System (PDS) to eligible households.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Income Certificate (if applicable)
Income Limit: Eligible households identified by State Government
Keywords: food security, ration card, subsidized food, PDS, food grains

Objective: Promote long-term savings for the education and marriage of girl children.
Description: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana is a savings scheme for the girl child that helps parents build funds for her education and future needs. It offers attractive interest rates and tax benefits to encourage long-term savings.
Eligibility: Parents or legal guardians of a girl child below 10 years of age can open an account in her name.
Benefits: High-interest savings, tax benefits, and financial security for the education and future needs of the girl child.
Required Documents: Birth Certificate of Girl Child, Aadhaar Card, Parent/Guardian ID, Address Proof
Age Criteria: Girl child below 10 years (at account opening)
Keywords: girl child, savings, education, marriage, investment, SSY

Objective: Improve the welfare, education, and survival of the girl child.
Description: BBBP promotes the protection, education, and empowerment of girls by addressing gender discrimination and improving the child sex ratio. It also encourages equal opportunities for girls in education and society.
Eligibility: The scheme benefits girls, women, and communities across India through awareness and government interventions; there is no individual application-based eligibility for most components.
Benefits: Awareness, education, and welfare initiatives promoting the protection and empowerment of the girl child.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card (where required), Birth Certificate (scheme-specific)
Keywords: girl child, women empowerment, education, awareness, gender equality

Objective: Empower women through safety, protection, and economic support initiatives.
Description: Mission Shakti is an integrated women's empowerment programme that provides safety, protection, welfare, and support services for women. It also promotes women's economic and social empowerment through various initiatives.
Eligibility: Women and girls eligible under the specific component of the scheme, such as safety, protection, or empowerment services, as per government guidelines.
Benefits: Support services for women including safety, protection, legal aid, counseling, skill development, and economic empowerment initiatives.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Identity Proof, Income Certificate (if applicable)
Income Limit: Varies by component (some services have no income limit)
Keywords: women safety, women empowerment, legal aid, counselling, support

Objective: Provide clean LPG cooking fuel to eligible women from poor households.
Description: PMUY provides free LPG connections to eligible women from poor households, replacing traditional cooking fuels. The scheme aims to improve health, reduce indoor air pollution, and promote clean cooking practices.
Eligibility: Adult women from eligible low-income households who do not already have an LPG connection and meet the scheme's beneficiary criteria.
Benefits: Free LPG connection with financial assistance for eligible households, promoting clean and smoke-free cooking.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Bank Account Details, Address Proof
Income Limit: Eligible low-income households as per government criteria
Keywords: LPG, gas connection, cooking gas, women, clean fuel, PMUY

Objective: Provide quality daycare facilities for children of working mothers.
Description: The National Creche Scheme provides daycare facilities for children of working mothers. It offers a safe environment with nutrition, healthcare, and early childhood education, enabling women to participate in the workforce.
Eligibility: Children aged 6 months to 6 years of working mothers from eligible families, as per scheme guidelines.
Benefits: Safe daycare, nutritious meals, healthcare, and early childhood education for children of working mothers.
Required Documents: Birth Certificate of Child, Aadhaar Card of Parent, Income Certificate (if applicable)
Income Limit: Preference to low-income families (state guidelines may apply)
Keywords: daycare, childcare, working mothers, children, nutrition

Objective: Improve nutrition and school attendance among children.
Description: PM Poshan provides nutritious cooked meals to students in government and government-aided schools. The scheme aims to improve children's nutritional status, increase school attendance, and reduce dropout rates.
Eligibility: Students enrolled in government, government-aided, and eligible local body schools from Classes I to VIII.
Benefits: Free nutritious cooked meals to school children, improving nutrition, attendance, and learning outcomes.
Required Documents: School Enrollment Record (maintained by school)
Keywords: mid-day meal, school meal, nutrition, students, education

Objective: Support meritorious students from economically weaker sections to continue education
Description: NMMSS provides financial assistance to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to help them continue secondary education. The scheme aims to reduce school dropouts due to financial constraints.
Eligibility: Meritorious students of Class VIII from economically weaker sections who qualify the selection examination and meet the prescribed income limit
Benefits: Annual scholarship to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to support secondary education.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, School Certificate, Bank Account Details
Income Limit: Annual family income up to ₹3.5 lakh
Keywords: scholarship, merit, students, financial aid, education

Objective: Provide a single platform for scholarship applications and disbursement.
Description: The National Scholarship Portal is a single online platform through which eligible students can apply for various government scholarships. It simplifies the application, verification, and scholarship disbursement process.
Eligibility: Students who satisfy the eligibility criteria of the specific scholarship, including academic performance, family income, and category requirements.
Benefits: Financial assistance through various government scholarships for eligible students pursuing school or higher education.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Previous Marksheet, Bank Account Details, Bonafide Certificate
Keywords: scholarship, education, student, financial assistance, NSP

Objective: Support education of meritorious students from OBC, EBC, and DNT communities.
Description: PM Yasasvi provides scholarships to meritorious students belonging to OBC, EBC, and DNT communities. The scheme supports quality education and promotes equal educational opportunities.
Eligibility: Meritorious students belonging to OBC, EBC, or DNT categories who meet the prescribed income and educational criteria.
Benefits: Scholarships to eligible OBC, EBC, and DNT students for continuing quality education.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Previous Marksheet
Income Limit: Annual family income up to ₹2.5 lak
Keywords: scholarship, OBC, EBC, DNT, students, education

Objective: Enhance employability through industry-relevant skill training.
Description: PMKVY offers free skill development training aligned with industry requirements. It enhances employability by providing certification and practical skills to youth across different sectors.
Eligibility: Indian youth meeting the age and educational requirements for the selected skill training course.
Benefits: Free skill training, industry-recognized certification, and improved employment opportunities.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Educational Certificates, Bank Account Details
Keywords: skill training, certification, employment, vocational training

Objective: Develop a skilled workforce to improve employment opportunities.
Description: Skill India Mission aims to equip youth with market-relevant skills through vocational education, training, and certification. It focuses on improving employability and supporting economic growth.
Eligibility: Indian citizens seeking vocational skill training and meeting the eligibility requirements of the chosen training programme.
Benefits: Vocational training, certification, and skill development to enhance employability and entrepreneurship.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Educational Qualification Certificate, Passport-size Photograph
Keywords: skills, training, employment, vocational education, youth

Objective: Promote apprenticeship training by supporting employers and apprentices.
Description: NAPS encourages employers to provide apprenticeship training by offering financial support and incentives. The scheme helps young people gain practical work experience and industry-relevant skills.
Eligibility: Individuals meeting the minimum educational qualifications and age requirements for apprenticeship training under participating establishments.
Benefits: Practical on-the-job training, stipend support, and industry experience through apprenticeships.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Educational Certificates, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph
Age Criteria: 14 years and above (18+ for hazardous trades)
Keywords: apprenticeship, internship, industry training, skill development

Objective: Provide students with practical experience in digital governance initiatives.
Description: The Digital India Internship Scheme provides students with hands-on experience in digital governance and emerging technologies by allowing them to work on government projects under expert guidance.
Eligibility: Students pursuing undergraduate, postgraduate, or research programmes from recognized institutions, meeting the eligibility criteria specified in the internship notification.
Benefits: Practical work experience, mentorship, and exposure to digital governance projects for students.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, College ID, Resume, Academic Transcripts
Keywords: internship, digital, government, students, technology

Objective: Create a national database and extend social security benefits to unorganized workers
Description: e-Shram creates a national database of unorganized workers to facilitate access to social security and welfare schemes. It helps workers receive benefits based on their occupation and eligibility.
Eligibility: Unorganized workers aged 16–59 years who are not income tax payers or members of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (subject to current scheme rules).
Benefits: Registration in a national database of unorganized workers, enabling access to social security and welfare schemes.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Mobile Number, Bank Account Details, Occupation Details
Keywords: unorganized workers, labour, social security, worker registration

Scheme Name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
Objective: Provide skill training and employment opportunities to rural youth.
Description: DDU-GKY provides skill development and placement-linked training to rural youth from poor households. The scheme aims to improve employability and create sustainable livelihood opportunities.
Eligibility: Rural youth aged 15–35 years from poor households identified under the scheme. Age relaxation applies for certain categories.
Benefits: Free skill training, placement assistance, certification, and improved employment opportunities for rural youth.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Educational Certificates, Bank Account Details
Income Limit: Poor rural households identified under government criteria
Keywords: rural youth, skill training, employment, placement, livelihood

Scheme Name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana – National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
Objective: Strengthen rural livelihoods through self-help groups and financial inclusion.
Description: DAY-NRLM strengthens rural livelihoods by organizing women into Self-Help Groups (SHGs), improving financial inclusion, and promoting income-generating activities. It focuses on reducing rural poverty through community participation
Eligibility: Rural poor households, especially women, willing to form or join Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and participate in livelihood activities.
Benefits: Formation of Self-Help Groups (SHGs), access to bank credit, skill development, and livelihood opportunities for rural households.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, SHG Membership Details (if applicable)
Keywords: SHG, women, rural livelihood, self-help group, bank loan

Objective: Ensure safe and adequate tap water supply to every rural household.
Description: Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide safe and adequate tap water to every rural household. The scheme focuses on improving drinking water infrastructure, water quality, and sustainable water management.
Eligibility: Rural households are covered through village-level implementation. Individual applications are generally not required.
Benefits: Safe and adequate tap water supply to rural households, improved sanitation, and better public health.
Required Documents: No individual documents required (implemented at village/community level)
Keywords: tap water, drinking water, rural water supply, sanitation

Objective: Improve sanitation and eliminate open defecation across India
Description: Swachh Bharat Mission promotes sanitation, hygiene, and proper waste management by encouraging toilet construction and eliminating open defecation. It aims to create cleaner and healthier communities
Eligibility: Rural and urban households, local bodies, institutions, and communities eligible under the mission's sanitation initiatives.
Benefits: Construction of toilets, improved sanitation facilities, cleaner surroundings, and better public health through waste management initiatives.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card (if applying for individual household benefits), Address Proof
Keywords: sanitation, toilet, cleanliness, hygiene, waste management

Scheme Name: AMRUT (Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation)
Objective: Improve urban infrastructure and basic services in cities.
Description: AMRUT improves urban infrastructure by enhancing water supply, sewerage systems, green spaces, and public amenities. The mission focuses on improving the quality of life in cities.
Eligibility: Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and cities selected under the mission. Citizens benefit through infrastructure projects rather than individual applications.
Benefits: Improved water supply, sewerage systems, urban green spaces, and better quality of urban infrastructure and services.
Required Documents: No individual application documents required (implemented through Urban Local Bodies)
Keywords: urban development, water supply, sewerage, infrastructure, parks

Objective: Transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
Description: Digital India aims to transform India into a digitally empowered society by expanding digital infrastructure, promoting online government services, and increasing digital literacy.
Eligibility: Citizens, businesses, educational institutions, and government organizations can access services under various Digital India initiatives, depending on the specific programme.
Benefits: Easy access to digital government services, improved internet connectivity, digital literacy, and online public service delivery.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card or Government-issued ID (for specific digital services, where applicable)
Keywords: digital services, internet, e-governance, digital literacy

Objective: Improve infrastructure planning and multimodal connectivity across India.
Description: PM Gati Shakti integrates infrastructure planning across different sectors to improve logistics, connectivity, and economic development. It supports faster and more coordinated infrastructure projects.
Eligibility: Central and State Government ministries, departments, and infrastructure agencies implementing approved infrastructure projects.
Benefits: Faster infrastructure development, improved logistics, better transport connectivity, and enhanced economic growth.
Required Documents: No individual application documents required (implemented by government agencies)
Keywords: infrastructure, logistics, transport, connectivity, development

Scheme Name: National Digital Health Mission (Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission)
Objective: Build a digital health ecosystem for efficient healthcare delivery.
Description: ABDM creates a digital healthcare ecosystem by providing digital health IDs, electronic health records, and seamless access to healthcare services. It promotes efficient and secure healthcare delivery.
Eligibility: All Indian citizens, healthcare professionals, and healthcare facilities can voluntarily register and participate in the digital health ecosystem.
Benefits: Digital Health ID, secure electronic health records, seamless access to healthcare services, and improved coordination among healthcare providers.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card or Mobile Number (for creating an ABHA ID), Basic Identity Details
Keywords: ABHA ID, digital health, health records, healthcare

Objective: Transform India's education system through equitable and quality learning.
Description: NEP 2020 reforms India's education system by promoting multidisciplinary learning, skill development, digital education, and improved access to quality education at all levels.
Eligibility: Students, teachers, educational institutions, and other stakeholders benefit through educational reforms implemented by governments and institutions.
Benefits: Improved quality of education, flexible learning pathways, multidisciplinary education, digital learning, and enhanced skill development.
Required Documents: Depends on the specific educational institution or programme; generally Aadhaar and academic documents where applicable
Keywords: education, NEP, school, higher education, skill development

Objective: Promote manufacturing, investment, and job creation in India.
Description: Make in India promotes domestic manufacturing, attracts investments, encourages innovation, and generates employment by making India a global manufacturing hub.
Eligibility: Domestic and foreign businesses, manufacturers, startups, and investors establishing or expanding manufacturing operations in India, subject to applicable regulations.
Benefits: Increased investment, promotion of domestic manufacturing, job creation, innovation, and economic growth.
Required Documents: Business Registration Documents, PAN Card, GST Registration (if applicable), Company Incorporation Documents
Keywords: manufacturing, investment, industry, jobs, business, entrepreneurship

Objective: Provide collateral-free loans to street vendors for business recovery and growth.
Description: PM SVANidhi provides collateral-free working capital loans to street vendors to help them restart or expand their businesses. The scheme also encourages digital transactions and timely loan repayment through incentives.
Eligibility: Street vendors engaged in vending before the notified cut-off date and recognized by the Urban Local Body or through other approved verification methods.
Benefits: Collateral-free working capital loans, interest subsidy on timely repayment, and cashback incentives for digital transactions.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, Vendor Certificate/ID, Bank Account Details, Mobile Number
Keywords: street vendor, vendor loan, working capital, small business, digital payment
